data.title: XPeng falls after seven sessions of gain
data.description:
XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) shares fell 2.88% to close at $23.13 on Friday, breaking a seven-session winning streak that had seen the stock climb steadily over the past week.

Despite today’s decline, shares remain up 14.31% over the past month and have surged 95.35% so far this year, significantly outperforming the broader market.

However, investor sentiment turned cautious after Tesla (TSLA) posted record third-quarter deliveries, beating expectations and setting a new quarterly high. Tesla’s surge was partly driven by a rush to secure the recently expired $7,500 federal EV tax credit. The company also reported strong demand in China, with its updated Model Y and new six-seat Model Y L variant capturing a growing share of the market.

Tesla’s dominant performance and plans to expand its footprint in China have raised concerns among investors about the competitive pressures facing local players that include Xpeng.

Meanwhile, analysts sentiment remain broadly positive. Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, XPEV holds a Hold recommendation with an average score of 3.3 out of 5. The company scored A+ in both growth and momentum, while receiving a D- for valuation and profitability. Revisions were rated B, indicating a generally favorable shift in outlook.

Seeking Alpha analysts rate the stock a Buy with an average score of 3.75.

Wall Street sentiment remains bullish, with 15 analysts giving the stock a Strong Buy, and 6 analysts recommending a Buy. Meanwhile, 6 analysts suggest holding the stock, and 1 has issued a Sell rating.

XPeng’s record September deliveries, up 94.7% year-over-year to 41,581 units, highlight its accelerating market share gains and solidify its position as a leading growth player in China’s competitive EV landscape.

However, an analyst says the rally is "well-deserved," pointing out the company's accelerating international growth, narrowing losses, and a potential bottom-line inflection, but advises caution as a pullback to the $19 range "may potentially occur."

data.title: Wall Street ended mixed as investors navigate the ongoing government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages closed mixed on Friday as market participants digested the U.S. government shutdown for the third day.

Pushing higher was the blue-chip Dow (DJI) as it closed up by 0.51%. Also, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished near even, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) suffered the most as it ended lower by 0.28%. 

For the week, the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite advanced by 1.1%, 1.08%, and 1.32%, respectively.

“Another very positive week in equities and crypto this week, federal government shutdown notwithstanding. The wall of worry is getting steeper, as calls of ‘this is the top!’ get louder and more commonplace.  But if the supply of money continues to increase, and the cost of money continues to fall, then most likely the price of risk assets continues to climb.  We continue to look higher into year end,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

On a sector-by-sector stance, sevenof the 11 S&P segments concluded higher, as Utilities led the way. In reverse, the Consumer Discretionary segment had struggled the most and was the weakest sector on the session. 

U.S. Treasury yields ticked higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 3 basis points to 4.12% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up 3 basis points to 3.57%.

The U.S. government shutdown began at midnight Wednesday after Republicans and Democrats failed to agree on a federal funding deal. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC on Thursday that the shutdown could weigh on GDP growth.

So far, Wall Street has largely brushed off the impasse. On prediction market Kalshi, the probability of the shutdown lasting more than 10 days rose to 69%.

With federal agencies closed, investors will be left without the September nonfarm payrolls report—a release widely viewed as critical for the Federal Reserve’s assessment of labor market conditions.

In other economic news, S&P Global US Services PMI business activity index came in at 54.2 in September, down from 54.5 in August.

The U.S. ISM Services Index came in at 50.0 in September from 52.0 in August, which was below the 51.6 consensus figure.

Under the magnificent seven umbrella, shares of Apple (AAPL) were downgraded by Jefferies to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones.

data.title: SA Roundtable: Will large cap stocks outperform small caps in Q4?
data.description:
We recently asked Seeking Alpha readers whether they thought large cap stocks would outperform small caps during the remainder of the year, or vice versa. 

Out of the 1,054 readers who responded to the survey, 27.1% said they thought large caps would be the winners, 26.1% picked small caps, 26.7% were optimistic about small and large caps, while 20.1% said they were pessimistic about both. 

 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Rick Orford, Agar Capital and Daniel Jones which stocks they thought would perform better in the year's last quarter.  

Rick Orford: I see the markets as being the most valued they've ever been - all this while economic and geopolitical issues are at their highest in generations. One only needs to look at Europe to see a place that was once unquestionably protected by its NATO peers now questioning whether that security will actually be provided if needed. Add to that looming interest rate cuts from the Fed on the back of weak employment and inflation data, and now we suddenly have reason to pause. 

One might argue that lower rates will help small cap stocks. Possibly. But right now, I see far more risks than rewards. 

For me, I'm holding. I'm bullish on the megacap companies with a long history of weathering storms and increasing their bottom line. Companies like Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), and Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) should continue to perform well regardless of what might happen. The defensive stocks should also do well, like Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG), Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO), and even names like Clorox (NYSE:CLX). Companies like these offer investors a good mix of what both the consumers and the government need.

Agar Capital: I remain constructive on large caps, but careful selection and choosing quality stocks is essential. The way I see it is that the Federal Reserve's rate reductions will have a ripple effect on the stock market, and consequently the shares from industrial and materials sectors, including technology, will also “join the party.” 

There are a lot of analysts drawing parallels to the 2000 dot-com bubble, but the growth of the stock market was quite different back then, as it was largely supported by debt and “creative finance"; companies were borrowing and using vendor financing to boost sales and inflate multiples. Today, the money circulating is real and put on the table by companies that are grinding out huge profits and by governments that are investing for strategic reasons. Valuations are high, but I wouldn't call it an AI bubble. Nevertheless, fuel is running out, and new catalysts are needed to push prices even higher, such as the quarterly reports that will start coming out in mid-October. 

Although I am not a fan of small caps, I am positive about this historic market phase. After the Fed's pivot, the Russell 2000 broke through its highs, even though investor positioning remains cautious. The Fed's rate cuts are very helpful to small caps, because they have a lot of short-term debt (average maturity 3 to 4 years) and each 25 bps rate cut translates into earnings relief of about 2 points.  

If the only focus was on companies with profits, stocks of small cap companies would be evaluated at their lower valuations by nearly 30% compared to large cap stocks, and this gap is significant since it provides the possibility of a return. Profit estimates are getting better, and the sentiment towards this area is not yet very positive, so there is potential for the move to go further. My advice would be to stay away from microcaps, as they are highly speculative.

Daniel Jones: I fully expect the gains that we have seen in recent months to not continue through the rest of this year. There was some optimism after it became clear that the Federal Reserve had finally started cutting interest rates again. In the long run, that will prove to be bullish. But in the short run, I expect the market to be guided more by legitimate economic uncertainties.  

The primary issue here is tariffs and the harmful impact they have had on the economic climate. Jobs data has been awful. Consumer confidence is near all-time lows. And a slew of other data suggests that we are heading toward a recession. And likely, it won't be just any recession. It will be one defined by stagflation.

I'm not sure if we will hit a recession this year. But since we are moving in that direction, I expect the market to start incorporating that data more and more. At best, this should mean that markets remain more or less flat. But there is a real chance that we could see a decline as we head into the new year. A low tide affects all boats, so I fully anticipate that large cap stocks and small cap stocks will experience pain.  

If I am correct about the direction things are heading, I expect large cap stocks to outperform small cap stocks. Over the long run, the opposite should be true. But there are two reasons for this mindset that I have. First and foremost, larger companies have a tendency, on average, to be more financially stable than smaller firms. They likely have less leverage and more levers at their disposal to deal with tough times. I view them as sort of a safe haven compared to smaller cap stocks.  

Of course, once things bottom out, small cap stocks are where the real opportunities lie. That needs to be taken into consideration, but I digress.  

The second reason for my way of thinking here, however, is that there are certain trends that are playing in favor of larger companies. Specifically, I'm talking about investments in AI and data centers. I don't expect, for instance, for firms like Facebook parent Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and Google parent Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) to be fundamentally impacted to a severe extent. There might be some weakness, such as around advertising spending. But beyond that, not much.  

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), which is another big player in this market, could see some pain on its ecommerce side. But with a lot of its value today driven by AWS and shares of the company not looking particularly expensive, it should help buoy the performance of large cap stocks as a group.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares, Veritex get all regulatory approvals for merger
data.description:
Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN) and Veritex Holdings (NASDAQ:VBTX) said the Federal Reserve Board of Governors and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency have approved the proposed merger of the two companies on Friday, paving the way to close the acquisition on Oct. 20, 2025.

The two approvals means the companies have received all the required regulatory approvals for the transaction, in which Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN) agreed to acquire Veritex (NASDAQ:VBTX) in an all-stock deal valued at ~$1.9B at the time the deal was first announced.

Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN) stock rose 1.0% in late Friday trading.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on the OpenAI event, Powell talk, PepsiCo earnings, and more IPOs
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - October 6

  • Volatility watch - Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) and Oklo (OKLO) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Tourmaline Bio (TRML), Vimeo (VMEO), and Novagold Resources (NG). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include WhiteHorse Finance (WHF), Kodiak AI (KDK), and Oxford Square (OXSQ).
  • IPO watch - Alliance Laundry (ALH) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade. The manufacturer of commercial laundry equipment based in Ripon, Wisconsin, plans to raise up to $751.2 million by offering roughly 34.1 million shares at a price range of $19 to $22 per share. Phoenix Education Partners (PXED) is also expected to start trading. The operator of the University of Phoenix aims to raise up to $140.3 million through an IPO of 4.25 million shares at a price range of $31 to $33 per share, which puts the post-IPO valuation at approximately $1.2 billion at the midpoint. The post-IPO quiet period ends for Via Transportation (VIA), Klarna Group (KLAR), Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB), Gemini Space Station (GEMI), and Figure Technology (FIGR) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Constellation Brands (STZ).
  • All day - OpenAI will hold its third annual OpenAI DevDay event at Fort Mason in San Francisco. Speakers will include Sam Altman.
  • All day - The eyes of the international gaming industry will be on the Global Gaming Expo trade show in Las Vegas. Participating companies include MGM Resorts International (MGM), Caesars Entertainment (CZR), Boyd Gaming Corporation (BYD), and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT).

 

Tuesday - October 7

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include McCormick (MKC).
  • Retail watch - The holiday shopping season will kick off with the two-day Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) Prime Deal Days event. Other large U.S. retailers are holding their own sales events during the same period to compete with Amazon Prime Deal Days, including Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Best Buy (BBY), Nordstrom (JWN), and Kohl's (KSS).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Ionis Pharmaceuticals' (IONS) Innovation Day, SailPoint (SAIL) presenting at the J.P. Morgan Software Forum, and Autodesk's (ADSK) digital Investor Day.
  • All day - The three-day SEMICON West 2025 will take place in Phoenix. Participating companies at the conference aimed at the global semiconductor industry include Intel (INTC), TSMC (TSM), Amkor, Applied Materials (AMAT), ASML (ASML), and NXP Semiconductors (NXPI).
  • All day - Atlassian (TEAM) will hold its Team 25 Europe three-day conference in Barcelona and online. The event centers on AI-powered teamwork, new product announcements, and showcases practical use cases for Atlassian tools in project management, IT service management, and digital collaboration.
  • 10:00 a.m. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in a discussion on the economic outlook at an event hosted by Fisk University.

 

Wednesday - October 8

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include AZZ (AZZ) and Bassett Furniture (BSET).
  • All day - Circana will release its August report on video game hardware and software sales. Investors will be watching for the impact of new games from EA Sports (EA) and Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) and the ongoing sales push from the summer's new Switch 2 console.
  • All day - Cadre (CDRE) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City. Honeywell International (HON) will host Solstice Investor Day.
  • 8:30 a.m. Shareholders of TaskUS (TASK) are scheduled to vote on take-private. 
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from its last meeting.

 

Thursday - October 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and Levi Strauss (LEVI). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Tilray (TLRY) after it reports.
  • All day - Ferrari (RACE) will reveal its first-ever all-electric vehicle during the company's Capital Markets Day in Maranello, Italy. The event will likely offer a detailed look at the technological underpinnings of the new EV, with CEO Benedetto Vigna hinting at an "innovative and unique" presentation.
  • All day - Notable investor events include CrediCorp (BAP) hosting its Investor Day and Elastic (ESTC) holding its Analyst Day
  • All day - The North American Blockchain Summit conference will take place in Dallas, Texas, as the Lone Star State looks to enhance its crypto-friendly reputation. The event will include discussions on Bitcoin, blockchain, and digital asset ecosystems.
  • 10:30 a.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will give welcome remarks before the Community Bank Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Board.
  • 11:00 a.m. Shares of MEG Energy (MEG:CA) will vote on the planned sale to Cenovus Energy (CVE).
  • 12:50 p.m. Paramount Skydance (PSKY) CEO David Ellison will give a talk at the Bloomberg Screen Time event on his vision for the newly merged company and where he’s looking to make more deals.
  • 3:45 p.m. Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman gives closing remarks before the Community Bank Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Board.

 

Friday - October 10

  • All day - Electronic Arts' (EA) Battlefield 6 launches for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC, with no early access, meaning all players begin at the same time globally. The game is set in 2027 and features a geopolitical conflict where a fractured NATO faces off against a private military group called Pax Armata.
  • All day - NBA preseason games will take place in Macau at The Venetian Arena. The Brooklyn Nets and Phoenix Suns will face each other for two games. The event is expected to provide a boost for The Venetian Macau, which is owned by Sands China (OTCPK:SCHYY), a majority-owned subsidiary of Las Vegas Sands (LVS).
  • All day - CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP), Precision BioSciences (DTIL), and Satellos Bioscience (OTC:MSCLF) are some of the notable companies presenting at the World Muscle Society Congress.
  • 9:45 a.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will give opening remarks and moderate a discussion before the 19th annual Community Bankers Symposium.
  • 10:00 a.m. The release of the preliminary University of Michigan survey for October will be closely watched by economists for the update on consumer sentiment and inflation expectations.

data.title: Utilities Duke Energy, Entergy rise as Scotiabank upgrades
data.description:
Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) +1.7% and Entergy (NYSE:ETR) +2.5% in Friday's trading as Scotiabank upgraded both utilities to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform with respective $137 and $105 price targets, while Southern Company (SO) and DTE Energy (DTE) are cut to Sector Perform with respective $99 and $147 PTs.

Scotiabank analyst Andrew Weisel sees Duke (NYSE:DUK) as uniquely-positioned among U.S. utilities in that its service territories have been seeing success with both data centers and manufacturers, headlined by Amazon's planned $10 billion investment in North Carolina to expand cloud computing infrastructure.

Weisel also noted supportive population growth in Duke's (DUK) service areas, with steady ~2% residential growth in the Carolinas, Florida and Indiana, and more broadly, Duke continues to expect load growth to accelerate, from 1.5%-2% in 2025-26 to 3%-4% in 2027-29, led by 4%-5% in the Carolinas in the outer years.

The analyst admitted being "late to the party" on Entergy (NYSE:ETR), but he sees the company as among the best positioned to capture additional data center wins, and perhaps more importantly, to convert incremental capex to upside to earnings, and credits management for the innovate arrangement with Meta in Louisiana that provides tremendous growth while protecting customers.

Weisel thinks there's more to come for Entergy (ETR) given the company’s excess capacity 
position - offering speed to market - low rates, and supportive and a proven regulatory and political environment, and while the stock is far from cheap, trading at a 15% P/E premium, "we're drawn to the fast growth, positive estimate revisions, and potential for more near-term catalysts, which we believe positions the stock well in today's risk-on macro environment."

data.title: Automaker stocks rise on report Trump considers heavy tariff relief
data.description:
Automaker stocks rose to session highs late in Friday's session after a report indicated President Trump is weighing "significant" tariff relief for U.S. assembly.

Volume jumped on General Motors (NYSE:GM), +1.7%; Ford Motor (NYSE:F), +3.4%; Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM), +2.1%; Stellantis (NYSE:STLA), +2.9%; and Honda Motor (NYSE:HMC), +2.8%. Shares in Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) remained 2% lower on the day.

Sen. Bernie Moreno told Reuters: "The signal to the car companies around the world is, look, you have final assembly in the U.S.; we’re going to reward you." 

"For Ford, for Toyota, for Honda, for Tesla, for GM, those are the, almost in order, the top five domestic content vehicle producers -- they'll be immune to tariffs," he added.

The report noted that Trump is considering maintaining an import adjustment offset at 3.75% for five years as well as expanding that offset to U.S. engine production, according to auto industry officials.

That offset didn't originally include engines, and was originally proposed to run through April 2026 ,to be followed by a second year at 2.5%

data.title: Tronox drops as J.P. Morgan downgrades on TiO2 industry weakness
data.description:
Tronox (NYSE:TROX) -0.3% in choppy trading Friday as J.P. Morgan downgraded shares to Neutral from Overweight, saying conditions in the titanium dioxide industry have become more difficult in the near term, as coatings companies continue to contend with poor domestic and European demand due to weak housing and construction markets.

JPM analyst Jeffrey Zekauskas said he had thought the implementation of tariffs in India and Brazil could lead to increased TiO2 demand in the near term and establish conditions for better future pricing, but delays and possible reversals in the implementation of tariffs, more difficult competitive conditions, and weaker than expected demand are leading to weaker than expected volumes and prices in the short term.

Zekauskas thinks Tronox's (NYSE:TROX) demand likely will be lower sequentially in Q3 given competitive conditions in the industry and weak end markets; the analyst said he is withdrawing his previous $7 price target for December 2026 "given uncertain and negative business conditions in the context of the good quality of Tronox’s long-term business franchise."

data.title: FDA introduces program to expedite review of domestic generic drugs
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has introduced a new pilot program aimed at accelerating the review process for generic drugs that are both tested and produced entirely within the United States.

This initiative, announced on Friday, seeks to promote domestic drug manufacturing and research.

Earlier this week, the FDA hosted a public meeting to outline its broader strategies to bolster U.S. pharmaceutical manufacturing.

According to the FDA, these measures are intended to enhance the U.S. drug supply chain and increase the availability of high-quality generic medications for American consumers.

data.title: The Taylor Swift Effect: The Showgirl breaks Spotify’s single-day steaming record
data.description:
Music giant Taylor Swift’s 12th studio album has broken the record of most single-day Spotify (NYSE:SPOT) streams for an album this year, according to the platform.

In less than 12 hours, “The Life of a Showgirl” has had the most plays on Spotify (NYSE:SPOT), after the album had already beaten the platform’s record for pre-saves at 5 million. Specific numbers for streaming of the new album have not yet been announced.

Target (TGT) is also the exclusive retailer for a limited copy of the vinyl version of the album.

Its previous studio album “The Tortured Poets Department” amassed more than 1.9M sold copies, according to Billboard.

“The Life of a Showgirl” was co-written by pop music experts Max Martin and Shellback, who Swift has previously worked with on hits like “Blank Space, “I Knew You Were Trouble,” and “Shake It Off.”

The “Eras tour” showgirl is also preparing a collaboration with AMC (AMC) Theaters Distribution and distributor Piece of Magic Entertainment to release a film title “TAYLOR SWIFT | THE OFFICIAL RELEASE PARTY OF A SHOWGIRL" in Latin America, Europe, and Asia to promote her album.

The film will be played in all AMC (AMC) locations in the U.S., in Cinemark (CNK), and Regal chains.

More on Taylor Swift:

  • AMC Entertainment reportedly collaborating with Taylor Swift for her 'secret' theatrical event
  • Taylor Swift and AMC tie up again for promo film distribution ahead of her album release
  • Taylor Swift's engagement leads to a spike in search interest for Ralph Lauren
  • The Taylor Swift Effect strikes again as her engagement announcement lifts Signet
